cent: noun. A subunit of currency equal to one-hundredth of the main unit of currency in many countries.
chest: noun. A box, now usually a large strong box with a secure convex lid.

cue: noun. The name of the Latin script letter Q / q.
cunt: noun. The female genitalia, especially the vulva.
cut: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Teutila Cuicatec.
cute: adjective. Possessing physical features, behaviors, personality traits or other properties that are mainly attributed to infants and small
cutest: adjective. superlative form of cute: most cute
ecu: noun. The European Currency Unit (symbol ₠), a currency used in the European Community before the euro.
ens: noun. An entity or being; an existing thing, as opposed to a quality or attribute.
hen: noun. A female chicken (Gallus gallus), especially a sexually mature one kept for her eggs.
hent: verb. To take hold of, to grasp.
hents: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of hent
hut: noun. A small, simple one- storey dwelling or shelter, often with just one room, and generally built of readily available local materials.
nest: noun. A structure built by a bird as a place to incubate eggs and rear young.
net: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nete.
nett: adjective. Alternative spelling of net (remaining after expenses or deductions).
nth: adjective. Occurring at position n in a sequence.
nus: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nuer.
nut: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nùng.
scut: noun. A hare; a hare as the game in a hunt.

sec: symbol. Symbol of the trigonometric function secant.
sect: noun. An offshoot of a larger religion or denomination.
sen: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nanerige.
sent: verb. simple past and past participle of send
she: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Sheko.
shent: verb. simple past and past participle of shend
shun: verb. To avoid, especially persistently; ostracize.
shunt: verb. To cause to move (suddenly), as by pushing or shoving; to give a (sudden) start to.
shut: verb. To close, in various senses.
stench: noun. a strong foul smell; a stink.

stun: verb. To incapacitate; especially by inducing disorientation or unconsciousness.

tench: noun. A species of freshwater game fish, Tinca tinca.
tet: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Tetum.
teth: noun. The ninth letter of many Semitic alphabets/abjads (Phoenician, Aramaic, Hebrew, Syriac, Arabic and others).
the: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Chitwania Tharu.
thus: adverb. In this way or manner.
